gloss_en: The apple is sweet and red.
notes: Apical ⟨s⟩ [s̺] 'sagarra' and trill ⟨rr⟩ [r] per hualde_lujanbio_zubiri2010; sibilant contrast per ladefoged_maddieson1996.

gloss_en: Seven white sheep are on the mountain.
notes: Laminal ⟨z⟩ [s̻] 'zazpi', 'zuri' — Upper Navarrese retains the three-way system per hualde_lujanbio_zubiri2010.

gloss_en: It is cold this morning.
The small dogs are in the house.
I drink cold water every day.
The sea is far from here.
The rooster is singing in the morning.
notes: Palatal lateral ⟨ll⟩ [ʎ] 'ollarra' and trill ⟨rr⟩ [r] per hualde_lujanbio_zubiri2010.

gloss_en: The child is sick in bed.
My heart is beating fast.
The teacher told us the tale.
notes: Palatal nasal ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ] 'andereñoak' per hualde_ortiz_de_urbina2003; sandhi 'ipuina kontatu'? coverage per hualde_lujanbio_zubiri2010.

sentence: Onddo eder bat hartu dugu basoan.
ipa: onɟo edeɾ bat haɾtu duɡu bas̺oan
gloss_en: We took a fine mushroom in the forest.
A drop has fallen to the ground.
notes: Palatal plosive ⟨tt⟩ [c] 'ttantta' and trill 'lurrera' per hualde_lujanbio_zubiri2010.

gloss_en: A little mouse runs by.
You must wash your hand.
Let us speak together.
The salt and pepper are on the table.
notes: Laminal affricate ⟨tz⟩ [ts̻] 'gatza', trill 'piperra' and ⟨h⟩ [h] 'mahaian' per saltarelli1988.

gloss_en: The tree is losing its leaves.
The girl and the boy walk together.
notes: Apical ⟨s⟩ [s̺] 'neska' and laminal affricate ⟨tz⟩ [ts̻] 'dabiltza' per saltarelli1988.

gloss_en: The castle stands on the hill.
notes: Laminal ⟨z⟩ [s̻] 'gaztelua' per hualde_lujanbio_zubiri2010; atonic-vowel coverage per saltarelli1988.

gloss_en: When do the village festivals begin?
